entries.statement: Pursue Helix modular-monolith with a staged invoice remediation track, paired with tracing and SLO gates, while keeping microservices extraction as a later phase if module boundaries fail.

basis.intent: Reduce checkout and invoice latency for enterprise accounts
supporting_evidence: P95 checkout latency remains above the 800ms target on peak days
Two lighthouse customers reported slowdowns during invoice generation rather than browsing
Checkout P95 was 940ms on Tuesday peak in last week's dashboard
assumptions_relied_on: Module boundaries will be ignored without tooling enforcement
Invoice batch guardrails can buy time if the backfill issue is fixed
risks_addressed: Operational overload if microservices are introduced too early
Incorrect bottleneck diagnosis leading to the wrong architecture move
risks_accepted: Vague Helix meaning slowing alignment and customer comms
constraints_respected: 14-person platform team with flat SRE headcount
tradeoffs: Architecture direction: Omar wants microservices extraction now vs Priya wants Helix modular monolith first
Near-term invoice remediation vs long-term re-architecture messaging
alternatives_considered: Extract payments and catalog services into microservices now
Stay on the monolith and invest only in performance guardrails
missing_context_caveats: Helix: internal platform codename, target modular-monolith shape, and migration program name are used interchangeably and remain unresolved
Northwind uptime promise expectations for modular-monolith

would_change_if: Profiling shows invoice latency is not the primary bottleneck.
Finance rejects the phased infrastructure budget envelope.
Northwind requires uptime promises the modular-monolith path cannot support.

entries.statement: Publish a one-page decision framing for exec staff

basis.intent: Present a phased architecture and cost story to finance and exec staff
constraints_respected: Finance wants phased infrastructure spend, not a big-bang budget hit

would_change_if: Exec staff requests a final architecture decision before receiving a framing note.

basis.intent: Reduce checkout and invoice latency for enterprise accounts
supporting_evidence: Invoice generation queue depth doubled during the backfill window on Thursday
assumptions_relied_on: Lighthouse slowdowns may reflect invoice generation rather than checkout alone
risks_addressed: Incorrect bottleneck diagnosis leading to the wrong architecture move
missing_context_caveats: Verified root cause for lighthouse slowdowns

would_change_if: Profiling shows checkout latency, not invoice generation, is the dominant bottleneck.

entries.statement: Define Helix contract boundaries and ownership

basis.intent: Enable safer independent deploys without overwhelming the platform team
assumptions_relied_on: Module boundaries will be ignored without tooling enforcement
risks_addressed: Vague Helix meaning slowing alignment and customer comms
missing_context_caveats: Helix: internal platform codename, target modular-monolith shape, and migration program name are used interchangeably and remain unresolved

would_change_if: The team agrees on a single Helix definition before contract work begins.

entries.statement: Model phased infrastructure cost for finance review

basis.intent: Present a phased architecture and cost story to finance and exec staff
constraints_respected: Finance wants phased infrastructure spend, not a big-bang budget hit

would_change_if: Finance provides a fixed budget envelope before modeling begins.

entries.statement: Add tracing coverage requirements before migration kickoff

basis.intent: Enable safer independent deploys without overwhelming the platform team
risks_addressed: Operational overload if microservices are introduced too early
constraints_respected: 14-person platform team with flat SRE headcount
tradeoffs: Independent deploy goals vs flat SRE headcount and on-call load

would_change_if: SRE capacity is confirmed insufficient even for a modular-monolith migration wave.

entries.statement: Draft customer messaging focused on remediation milestones rather than architecture jargon

basis.intent: Meet a credible September performance remediation milestone
supporting_evidence: Northwind asked whether modular-monolith means the same uptime promises and the team did not answer
constraints_respected: Sales messaging must not promise a rewrite timeline the team cannot keep
tradeoffs: Near-term invoice remediation vs long-term re-architecture messaging
missing_context_caveats: Northwind uptime promise expectations for modular-monolith

would_change_if: Customer success confirms September messaging can reference architecture changes directly.
